text.title: Backup Verification + Disaster Recovery Drills Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Operator declares 'backups are running' for 2 years; first restore attempt finds 18 months of WAL gap and unreadable tar files.
antipattern.root-cause: No automated drill was scheduled; manual quarterly drills slipped repeatedly.
antipattern.fix: Schedule a weekly restore-drill cron; have it fail loudly via PagerDuty on non-zero exit; require drill-pass before approving release.

antipattern.symptom: Monitor checks 'file exists in S3'; a partial 0-byte upload passes the check while the restore fails.
antipattern.root-cause: Monitor was wired to object existence, not size or restore exit code.
antipattern.fix: Replace existence check with `backup_size_bytes` + median-anomaly alert + scheduled restore-drill with exit-code propagation.

antipattern.symptom: DR runbook lives in a wiki with no version control; during incident the on-call discovers half the commands reference a decommissioned server.
antipattern.root-cause: Runbook was authored once and never refreshed; no automation to detect drift.
antipattern.fix: Move runbook to Markdown in the repo (see `inc-runbook-as-markdown-tagged-steps`); add weekly cron that lints command syntax + dry-runs each tagged step.
